title: phlox speciosa var. sabinii
intro: Phlox speciosa var. sabinii, also known as Sabin's phlox or Sabin's bee balm, is a fragrant, flowering plant native to the western United States. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds, sore throats, and digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and promote a healthy immune system. Phlox speciosa var. sabinii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, and its dried flowers and leaves can be used in potpourri and perfumes.
